reasoning: The UAH satellite temperature anomaly for October 2025 was reported at +0.53°C (relative to the 1991-2020 baseline). NASA GISS uses a 1951-1980 baseline, which typically sits about 0.6°C to 0.7°C cooler than the 1991-2020 average. This gives an expected NASA GISS anomaly around 1.13°C to 1.23°C. The bracket in question (1.10°C to 1.14°C) is very narrow (only 0.05°C wide) and covers the lower portion of the most probable range. Because Polymarket global temperature markets typically feature a half-dozen or more contiguous 0.05°C brackets (e.g., 1.05-1.09, 1.10-1.14, 1.15-1.19, 1.20-1.24), and this particular bracket is slightly below the center of the expected value based on the UAH proxy, the probability of the final NASA figure landing precisely in this bracket is relatively low, around 16%.
batch_queries: Will global temperature increase by between 1.10ºC and 1.14ºC in October 2025?
